TextENGNew: "...Seems we're not going back empty-handed thanks to this."
Chief Oouchi of the National Research Institute of Police Science (NRIPS) closed the investigation report he just finished reading as he said that.
Then he nodded and said "thank you for your assistance," while sliding the documents across the desk to Section Chief Okabe of the Second Criminal Investigation Division of Kakiuchi Station.
"Oh, no, this is nothing.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1002. Anyway, I didn't want it to be unnecessary baggage, so I tried to focus on just the format...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1003. So, is it of any use?"
"Don't be absurd.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1003. I've been with NRIPS a long time... and I've never seen a document summarized with this much detail."
"So much that I'd like to give those university professors who do nothing but sit back while collecting an exorbitant salary a taste of their medicine."
"Why thank you.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1005. Everyone who worked so hard to assemble these materials will be pleased to hear that."
Okabe let out a hearty "wahaha" laugh as he picked up the documents.

In fact, these materials were only completed this morning. He had very little time for sleep the past few days, but he heard that Oouchi was returning to Tokyo for a while starting tomorrow, so he was forced to compile them.
...Of course, he couldn't exactly explain that and expect gratitude. No matter what he said, he wouldn't understand.
At best, he could boast to his colleagues about the reward he received... Okabe muttered that to himself while staring at Oouchi.
"The profiling analysis pilot project is very important to NRIPS HQ."
"We don't have a lot of opportunities to do this sort of work without the help of on-site investigators.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1007. The other researchers have their fingers crossed."
"Is that so? Great."

"No matter how much we analyze old case files, in the end we're simply evaluating antiques.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1009. The data we have is just compiled based on prejudiced assessment of inexperienced investigators."
"But if we had nothing but competent people like the investigators this time, it could prove more useful."
"...I'm flattered."
While chiming in with generally agreeable responses, he nearly said "rude bastard", only to swallow his words.
Applying current values to the past is arrogant and presumptuous in the first place. They did the best they could at the time, with their limited know-how and technology.
(In that case, try doing it yourself.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1009. Not that you even can...)

Plus, Oouchi treats actual investigations like research samples, and likes to think he's controlling the investigators like he's simply moving around game pieces... How imprudent, considering real people have died.
It was rather aggravating, but these sorts of "prodigies" have a remarkable lack of respect and consideration for others when speaking of them.
Perhaps in their minds they see the thoughts and feelings of the victims, the criminals, and other people involved as nothing but inorganic data meant to be quantified.
That's why detectives in the field don't think highly of NRIPS. ...NRIPS probably doesn't even realize it, and never will.
(...If it weren't for all the face I need to save, I'd kick his ass no questions asked...)
To be honest, he had no duty or reason to do this much for them. Investigation materials are valuable records for detectives in the first place.
Nevertheless, he wasn't proud to be providing free information on a moment's notice to the NRIPS guys who just take materials from other departments and do their work from the safety of their desks.
(...Still.)
Okabe and the others in the Second Investigation Division took a heavy blow to their reputation when they were pulled off a joint investigation with HQ last month and replaced by the First Division.
Since Deputy Chief Hata had ordered him to take advantage of this opportunity to save face, he wasn't in much of a position to refuse.
But if it meant being pushed around by this youngster so lacking in common sense, acting like a bigger man than he really was, then Okabe would rather be arguing with that "little girl" again...
(You idiot... What are you even saying?)
Okabe muttered that to himself, then shook his head to stave off his idle thoughts.
Oouchi's proposal to commit a breach of mistrust against that "little girl" should have been a godsend to Okabe, what with his own repulsion of her.
So what's the point in regretting it now...?
Okabe tried hard to fight off the guilt swelling in his heart, while scolding himself.
Because if he couldn't do that... then his pride would shrink even smaller.
"Oh, by the way,@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1014. how is the joint investigation with headquarters progressing?"

"Hmm... there have been difficulties.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1012. After all, it's been half a year since the incident occurred, and they didn't start out with any useful clues.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1013. Plus, they're having a hard time digging up the memories of the people involved."
"Hoh...?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1016. So, the situation hasn't changed much."
"Hahaha, that's right.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1015. Well, the deadline's not too far off, so we'll do everything we can until then."
(...This is no time for laughing.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1018. Doesn't he know what he came here for?)
He was irked by Oouchi's attitude and how he talked about it like it was someone else's problem, but he pretended not to notice.
...Three months had passed since NRIPS assembled a special team to participate in the joint investigation into the Oumaki schoolgirl murder case.

It's currently being led by the First Investigation Division at prefectural police HQ and the First Division at Kakiuchi Station, since Okabe and the Second Division were pulled off the case and forced to resume normal operations.
However, despite daily investigation and regular staff rotations, they still hadn't exhausted their list of suspects. ...Or rather, they couldn't even assemble a list of suspicious individuals.

And for now, on top of the constant investigation and gathering of personal information, NRIPS had laid out a manual outlining investigation methodologies. But despite all that, things have still been progressing slowly.
Although, it's natural for there to be difficulties finding meaningful evidence now that half a year has passed since the incident.
However... Oouchi and the guys from NRIPS forcibly cut into the investigation in an effort to identify a culprit.
Nevertheless, there hasn't been any significant increase in progress despite the additional manpower.
Even though it was someone else's problem, when Okabe saw that he wasn't sure if he should be more embarrassed or feel a sense of impending crisis.
(Well, he did find a different "feeding ground" in exchange.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1020. So he's probably not interested anymore.)
Yes... he understood well the reason Oouchi could make that face. Because there had been some surprising results.

The truth is, he made arrangements to have a search team observe the implementation of the NRIPS-assembled investigation manual, and it was approved separately from the joint investigation headquarters project.
And he secretly colluded with the Second Division, using surplus staff to monitor and verify how effective the process is in real-world on-site investigation over the past six months.
Ultimately, they were able to investigate some suspicious people according to the manual... and were able to crack down on a large number of minor crimes as a results.
"We also gained some insight into when and where crime was likely to occur.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1017. It was a surprise to learn that the weather and temperature had an effect."

"Indeed...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1022. Honestly, I was skeptical at first.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1023. Giving me a manual's all well and good, but I thought it was a bunch of crap while I was using it."
But when they opened the lid... amazing results came out.
Minor crimes like theft and assault are very difficult to deter, and investigation results were poor using the original methodology.
Until now, the only way to identify the suspect was through careful inquiry and eyewitness testimony. Otherwise, undercover agents would have to catch the criminal in the act.
However, this method of identifying suspects from suspicious individuals is both accurate and realistic... Just the other day, it even succeeded in catching a suspect right before the crime occurred.
What was most shocking of all to battle-hardened Okabe was that the perpetrator was captured by an inexperienced young detective.

"...Figure out where the guys planning the crimes are and take action.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1025. And then figure out what escape route they'll end up taking.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1026. When the investigators followed the manual, it was right on the money."
"Thanks to that, habitual criminals who have been at it for a long time were easily caught in the net.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1028. The people being arrested were the most surprised of all."

"...As to be expected from western criminal psychology.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1019. It's about thoroughly pursuing the means to increase the efficiency and usefulness of investigations.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1020. It was just as much a revelation for us, too."
"These results have been highly evaluated by the central government, and will result in a future review of our investigation system..."
(...Come on now, no need to brag.)
Okabe was frustrated with Oouchi's attitude, acting like this was all thanks to them.
It's certainly true that Okabe and the on-site investigators thought highly of this investigation manual.
It was the latest theory that western countries were using to incorporate scientific analysis into fighting crime... Profiling.
It was both surprising and confusing for the old guard detectives who had relied primarily on their intuition and years of experience.
(If this manual really does get enacted, it'll make a huge difference...)
Intuition, experience, and consistent inquiry are the key elements of an investigation.
...But it's impossible to learn those skills overnight. It takes time and effort to train young detectives to reach that point.
But what would happen if this manual is adopted at full-scale?
Up until now, young investigators could only be used for information gathering, but now they can accurately hunt down suspects or even catch them in their nets.
Their nets have gotten finer, so to speak, and it's no exaggeration to say they're able to aim those nets in places where prey is more densely populated.
...Of course, there are always exceptions to any rule, so relying exclusively on the theory could lead to unexpected missteps.
But that hole can be filled through the experience of veterans. This will increase the diversity of police investigations, and more importantly, increase their ability to apprehend criminals...

"...The police don't need the words "good old days."@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1032. We do our best to apprehend criminals for the safety of our citizens.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1033. As long as it accomplishes that, we're not picky about the methods."
Okabe always sourly said that to his detectives.
Criminals are getting smarter year after year, improving their skills at evading the net of the law.
And the police need to acquire the skills to stay on top of them.
Doing so is necessary to protect the dignity of the police force...
Any investigator who has survived the trial of fire on the scene has felt it, both large and small.
(Everyone knows it.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1035. ...But, there was always a high hurdle introducing it to them.)
Even if the western theories are good, just translating them doesn't help. Because Japan has different lifestyle and crime patterns.

Thus, in order to apply it in a Japanese setting, you need an experienced person to conduct the analysis. Someone who understands the feel of the crime scene, but also understands Japan's unique geography and means of transportation.

Moreover, the folks at NRIPS who always view the material from behind their desks, who've never felt the sorrow and hatred of the people involved in these incidents, would take decades to make anything tangible out of it.
Even if they put it all together and distributed it, it would end up laughed off as a useless empty theory, and they'd have no choice but to toss it in the trash.
This material was organized by the section chief of the Ibaraki prefectural police, based on long-term investigation data. Okabe had read the dissertation before... with a fine eye on the details.

And it was carefully summarized even while in the midst of conducting hard work. It was undoubtedly a result of that "little girl" putting in the effort to create a theoretical manual that caught the eye of upper management...
"It would be great if the Oumaki culprit could be caught this way...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1023. Anyway, just being able to bring results back at all is a good first step, right?"

"This will be a great help to investigators around the country, as a way to improve investigative activities in the future.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_oouchi1025. All the gratitude in the world wouldn't be enough to thank you, Section Chief Okabe."
"...I'm flattered.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1037. Now, I must be taking my leave."
After a bow, Okabe turned on his heels and left the room. ...If this conversation went on any further, he was seriously about to punch the guy.
After returning to the Second Investigation Division office, he plopped down on his seat and threw his feet up on the desk.
...It was a habit Okabe resorted to when he was in a really bad mood. So the detectives around him knew it was a sign to let sleeping dogs lie, and they left him be.
"...I have no idea how that little girl can put up with those bastards."
Okabe muttered that to himself after sucking down three cigarettes to quell his anger.
They were feigning compliance, but Oouchi and the others were largely ignoring joint investigation HQ's use of the manual and instead trying to win some success on other cases.
For Oouchi, incidents are the place to earn career advancement points, so naturally he was careful to choose the ones that looked most promising and give them precedence.
However... he didn't realize that this specific characteristic was causing detectives in the field to resent him.
(That girl keeps running around for that trash.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1040. ...Why?)
That "little girl" probably already knew about Oouchi's betrayal. Nevertheless... she accepted it and kept going.
On the contrary, she went to prefectural police HQ several times in the name of cooperation with Public Relations ever since NRIPS launched this project.
Working with the joint investigation detectives in the field, taking the time to explain things carefully, and acting as the mediator, without letting it hurt her feelings or her pride.
Whenever the NRIPS staff made improper remarks or gave insolent orders, she'd simply bow her head instead of retaliating.
On top of that, she would regularly visit the site to gather opinions and see if there were any pain points among the investigators... and tried to appease them.
That "little girl" spared no effort, even when it wouldn't help her evaluation and would make her an outcast... That was Tomoe Minai.
She doing backbreaking work that even athletic men would gripe about, without a word of complaint. Even Okabe who hated her was in awe of her dedication.
(Even when she's in a disadvantaged position, she's still succeeding...)
That reminded Okabe of an earlier conversation he had with the section chief of the first division, Doujima.
Doujima said that he actually refused to cooperate when Tomoe first came here to explain the NRIPS project.

"...I understand how you feel, Princess.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1003. I heard about the Oumaki incident from Hayami, and I completely understand why you'd want to start something with that bastard Okabe."
"...But this is a totally different story.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1005. Reckless retaliation is a completely selfish move.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1006. You realize that, don't you?"

"Yes, I understand that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1353. Pushing forward out of hatred or personal grudges will get you nowhere.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1354. Not to mention, I'd be opposed to mobilizing such a large number of people across multiple organizations over a personal matter."

"Then, why...?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1008. We know absolutely nothing about the culprit or their motivation. And clues have completely dried up.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1009. You told the press we'd find the solution in three months... Why such an unreasonable deadline?"
"I'm aware it's unreasonable.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1356. Honestly, I'm not confident I can reach that goal.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1357. ...But, I want to stand up and fight."
"I believe there is a powerful will lurking behind this case."
"If we give up and close the investigation before we uncover that, we're allowing an evil existence to walk free, which could shake the very nature of the police enforcing justice."
"As a police officer, I need to be a model citizen. I want to do everything in my power to fight evil."
It was just as Tomoe said. There were far too many anomalous elements to this case.
A schoolgirl assaulted her classmates while shouting about "the curse of Oyashiro-sama."

One of the victims was hospitalized after the incident, and was later murdered in a way the killer attempted to portray as suicide. Then that murderer went on to commit suicide right after offering a mysterious confession.
On top of that, another girl who had close relations with the victim died in very suspicious circumstances...
It certainly had a different feel to it than your typical impulsive act of violence. However...
"...There are countless unresolved cases in this country.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1011. This particular case is merely one of them."
"Rather than wasting time on this, wouldn't we be better off focusing on something we can actually solve?"

"It's just as you say.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1362. There are plenty of cases, big and small, and none of them have any special priority.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1363. ...That's why I just want to focus on the case that's right in front of me."
If she compromised here, next time she faced a difficult case, somewhere in her mind she'd feel the urge to "run away."
...Tomoe couldn't stand that.
No matter how much disappointment and frustration she faced... she wanted to fight it with all her might, and get results.
She vowed to always pour her heart toward that goal, because otherwise she couldn't forgive herself.
"So, this is the project that got even NRIPS to act, huh...?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1014. Anyway, are you sure you should have flaunted it right before the mass media?"

"No matter how much you might want to demonstrate profiling analysis to upper management, if it fails you'll be in serious trouble.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1016. Your own path... no, even your escape route might be cut off, you know?"

"Our enemy is something huge, completely lurking in the darkness.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1365. So, it's unlikely that conventional investigation will be enough to expose their true colors.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1366. In which case, it's better to do something flashy and see what results it brings.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1367. ...That's what the mass media is for."

"...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1018.Even so, this is a gamble with poor odds.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1019. In fact, all I can think is that those odds are completely desperate.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1020. Princess, you probably know that better than anyone, don't you?"
Solving a cold case in just three months...

To put it plainly, the odds are slim... In fact, the success rate is low enough it might as well be considered impossible. It's hard to believe that the big shots even had enough faith to authorize it.
However.

"The more spectacular the prize, the more likely they are to bite, right?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1369. Plus, it's not like hunting buried treasure or mysterious ruins... Even if we don't find the truth, just pursuing it puts on enough of a show on its own."
"The TV stations don't really expect success either... the rest is based purely on my determination."
"Princess, you..."
Knowing her intentions behind this, Doujima was stunned.
She shining a light on the incident to attract the attention of the mass media... and using that pretense to gather information on the case.
And she even dragged NRIPS into this, hoping to give the enemy a sense of crisis, driving them to panic and change their behavior.
Even if the investigation failed... the only damage would be to Tomoe's career. The TV stations wouldn't care as long as they got the ratings they wanted.
On top of that, she's putting her own life at risk by volunteering for the role of the decoy...
Programming about extreme and heroic events has major wide-audience appeal. If the developments or the ending lead to tragedy, that'll give it even more attention.
And because it's not a work of fiction but an actual documentary, there's no need for nosy directing or excessive tension. On the station side of things, it was a very appetizing theme.
"...And if you were to die, that would be even better for the ratings.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1023. Are you going to make me leave commentary at the memorial broadcast?"
"No way, I have no intention of letting things go that far."

"But the program will be exciting enough as-is, and public interest will at least be higher than regular coverage of police activity.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1373. That way, there's no risk of this case fading away."
"Indeed, that's certainly true, but..."
...Once an incident stops being fresh and topical, investigators obtain far less information through questioning.
In other words, Tomoe was trying to keep the investigation efficiency up by consistently making the case topical.
Doujima thoroughly understood that this was an act of "police absurdity." He was amazed at her and had to hold back his admiration, so he suddenly let out a laugh...
"I completely understand how you feel.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1026. I'll do everything in my power to cooperate.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1027. But... don't blame me if it goes poorly."
"Yes, as you wish."
Tomoe smiled as she said that, and her smile was full of courage, resolution, and strong will....
"...That said,@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1029. I also understand that this is no easy task.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_douji1030. I think her determination really is something to be proud of."
"...Hmph."
As he bitterly listened to Doujima's enthusiastic declaration... Okabe suddenly noticed an emotion welling up inside his heart different from his instinctual hatred.
Mixed feelings, ranging between admiration and jealousy, upon seeing someone pushing hard to make something happen that he'd once given up on.
In a sense... maybe it was envy...
(...That's just how much those greenhorns at NRIPS are sticking their noses in things.)
At this point... Oouchi and the guys at NRIPS were no longer interested in investigating the Oumaki schoolgirl murder incident.
Did they decide in their preliminary investigation that the case was unsolvable...? They seemed to give up early on, and all their subsequent investigative activities were no more than a formality.
In fact, they seemed to place much more importance on the "minor crimes action manual" that Deputy Chief Hata assembled in secret, in direct defiance of joint investigation HQ.

Instead of trying to gain credit by solving an unsolvable case, he would earn points by collecting data that can be easily and reliably gathered... That seemed the more prudent means of self-preservation for a thin-skinned bookworm.
Looking at them with that attitude, even Okabe who wasn't involved with investigative HQ felt astonished and thought to himself "what did they even come here to do?"
But... Tomoe and her colleagues may have already learned about Oouchi's "inside job." And on top of that, they respect their position as mediators as a matter of convenience.
And in addition to being acknowledged by field investigators, she had the pride of a top-class elite. ...Just imagining the amount of labor involved was headache inducing.
That's why she had such a strong belief in catching the culprit... But it would be impossible to endure that without putting all your energy into it.
"...Naturally, Asami would be devoted to that too."
His head finally starting to cool off, Okabe muttered that and smiled.
Asami became Deputy Section Chief of the Second Division after Kojima retired to take care of his parents. He transferred from a detective position at prefectural HQ.
Unlike his predecessor, he was sharp and quick to take action, which earned him several awards from the chief of headquarters.
So, Okabe saw him as a nearly perfect right-hand man... The only thing he didn't like was how friendly he was with Tomoe.
"...To be honest, it feels like this side is arbitrarily putting up a wall."
Indeed, when they were drinking together at a social gathering, Asami recognized Okabe's sour mood and vehemently declared it.
"That manual on profiling analysis is really well done."
"I've read several papers on the topic, but never seen anything that so accurately summarized it and applied it to the circumstances of Japanese investigations."
"I want to make this mandatory training material, not just for the new recruits, but for veterans too."

"Ridiculous...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1047. It's hard to think someone who's been around as long as you has been taken in by a document put together by a greenhorn.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1048. Everything we need is already committed to memory in our very bodies."

"Yes... it's true that this writing is based heavily on the knowledge and intuition we've learned through experience.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1006. ...Really, it's an investigative technique that we "alone" currently possess."
"But with this, we're able to pass it on to our subordinates and successors."
"That's still an armchair theory.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_okabe1050. You can line up the men of talent, but what worked is by chance."

"...Still, most of the staff in the Second Division are going through the manual right now.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1009. They're asking people to lend them theirs, or to make them a copy.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1010. ...It's ridiculously popular."

Detective working conditions are generally harsh. Working late at night and early in the morning... Sometimes you need to stay vigilant in a post for days at a time, and even when you return home that doesn't mean you'll be fully rested.
To make it worse, rookie detectives get practically no days off, and even when they do get a break it's barely enough to heal the mental and physical fatigue.
These same detectives... they say they're trying to make time to read the manual during meals or right before bed, reading carefully to drill the contents into their heads.
And they're consulting with veteran detectives about how to interpret and apply the content, or asking them to point out flaws and inconsistencies, which is making for a lively workplace atmosphere.
"Until now, we've been competing on an individual level on how to catch criminals more efficiently."
"Of course, I also believed in passing down knowledge and techniques, but somewhere in my heart I felt reluctant to so easily hand down the skills I struggled to obtain."

"...But, that Minai is something completely different.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1014. All she thinks about is catching criminals and carrying out justice.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_asami1015. When I think of that... I'm really glad to have a person like that."
"That Asami... he's a youngster, himself."
He was trying to convince himself of that... but even Okabe didn't believe that.
There are things that we couldn't do even if we wanted to. Or that we gave up on. ...Trying to shape it, little by little, still struggling but always looking forward.
For better or worse, her presence undoubtedly changed Kakiuchi Station.
Kakiuchi Station had to increase personnel to handle the recent population surge in the booming commuter town. The station had become a collision zone of the old and new since the reorganization.
Those who worked here a long time laid claim to their vested interests, and the newcomers fought hard to secure their place.

Thus, the newcomers never stopped being motivated and the veterans continued protecting themselves... It's no exaggeration to say that, as an organization, they were experiencing a clogging of the arteries.
A young woman came to Kakiuchi Station half a year ago, Tomoe Minai.

Her presence was initially taken with a lot of curiosity, or careful scrutiny... but now, she had gained the compassion and support of the majority of the people here, and they had come to rely on her.
Also, the people opposed to her ultimately gave up on their various schemes. And they too played a role in invigorating the organization.
Yes, you could perhaps say Tomoe's talent wasn't all that outstanding.
...Still, she inspired a trend. Her presence, which answered the expectations of those in Kakiuchi Station who had latently desired "change," was by no means trivial.
"Princess... huh?"
Every time he heard that name, Okabe felt unpleasant.
But recently, he began thinking... was that feeling really displeasure?
Or maybe...
(...It's about time.)
Madoka looked at her watch and took a sip from her second oolong tea of the day to calm herself down.
Thanks to that, her stomach was totally bloated... and yet her throat was completely dry.
"They said it was going to be a long meeting, but still..."
Madoka heard about it this morning, Tomoe was scheduled to have coverage at the TV station after this meeting. She loathed being late, so she'd probably leave sooner or later.
Madoka figured that Tomoe would want to quickly catch a cold drink beforehand, though. ...But knowing Tomoe, she wouldn't have someone make one for her or go out to buy it for her.
Which is why she'd come to this vending machine to buy something for herself. ...That was her chance.
"Haah, so hot..."
(...She's here...!)
Sure enough, Tomoe came into the break room patting her hands together. Madoka quietly cheered to herself... but quickly remembered the reason she was waiting and refocused herself.
"...Huh?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1377. Madoka, you're here?"
"Yep.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1005. G-great work today."
"You too.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1379. ...Wait, aren't you supposed to be on patrol at this hour?"
"Gh...!@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1007. H...how do you know my schedule?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1008. Who told you?"
"Ozawa-san in the traffic division.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1381. He said you were grumbling about having to work when you already had plans to go shopping."
(That ass~, I told him to keep it secret.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1010. ...I'll give him a piece of my mind later.)
She cursed him out for this unexpected development, but decided to let it go. If she said something here, Tomoe would probably launch into a scolding and their time would be up.
There was no time to waste here, not even a second.
Because Madoka was just about to ask a once-in-a-lifetime humble request...

"Ah, ahahaha...@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1012. I just had a bit of desk work to take care of.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1013. Oh, but I'm going to switch back to the normal schedule starting next week, so it's okay."
"Oh yeah...?"

Tomoe seemed like she wanted to say more, but for the time being she went over to the vending machine and pushed the button for a coffee. A loud rattle rang out as the cold can rolled down to the pickup slot.
Madoka clenched her fists to inspire herself.
That's right, women are courageous. ...Or was it affable?
Wait, that doesn't matter.
"H-hey... Sis.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1016. Umm."
"...You wanted to talk about something, right?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1384. With me."
She thinking about lightly introducing the topic... but Tomoe suddenly cut in and made the first strike.
And with Madoka unable to muster an answer, she took a seat on the sofa and started rolling the cold coffee around in the palm of her hand.

"You have a certain habit.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1386. Whenever you're planning to talk about something unreasonable, you hide your thumbs inside your clasped hands.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1387. I thought it was amusing, so I always remembered it."
She hid her hands behind her back, but it was too late. ...She had already lost the initiative.
Although, Tomoe was still smiling for the moment... Somehow, she didn't seem to be in a bad mood.
...But, she was staring straight with a cold, painful gaze. Or was that just her imagination? ...No, probably not.
"I have plans after this, so I don't have a lot of time...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1389. Anyway, I'm listening.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1390. You wanted to talk about something?"
"Ah... yeah.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1022. I guess... you could say that."
She had no choice. She had to prepare herself.
...Muttering that to herself, Madoka sat the can in her hand down on the table in front of her.
Then, she straightened her posture with determination, turned toward Tomoe, and took a deep breath before squeezing the words out.
"So... actually... umm...@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1024. Kaoru's parents are coming to Kakiuchi from Yamaguchi some time near the end of June..."
"Umm... so, when they get here, it'll be a rare opportunity for us to meet up as a family..."
Tomoe remained silent and took a sip from her can of coffee. ...Struggling to maintain eye contact, Madoka looked down and played with her fingers.
Meeting face-to-face as a family. ...Needless to say, it was a solemn ritual that individuals normally conducted when two or three steps away from engagement.
And right here, right on the spot... she was asking her sister, who was staunchly opposed to her marriage... to sit beside her at this gathering?
...She couldn't think of anything to say. It was mission impossible.
When Yamaoki entrusted Madoka with the role of asking Tomoe about this, she seriously considered asking Fujita or Hanada for help (or protection)...
...So heavy.
The air felt incredibly heavy.
In the end, maybe she should have asked about it another day, at a less sudden and confusing time...?
Still, the longer she put it off, the less time it would give her to persuade her...
No, she can't get a good answer in a situation like this without laying down sufficient groundwork, right...?
Thinking that, she was about to say "N-nevermind, forget it!" ...But just then.
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1395.When?"
"Huh?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1030. P-probably some time two weeks from now..."
"Two weeks from now, huh...?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1397. Thursdays are definitely tough, but..."
As soon as she heard that reply, Madoka looked up with her mouth open wide.
When she looked at Tomoe, she was pulling the police notebook out of her breast pocket to check her schedule.

"I thought it might be something like that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1399. ...It's fine, I can go.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1400. I do have something to attend to on Thursdays, so another day would be preferable."
"H...huh...?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1034. Th-then...?"

"They're coming all the way out here, right?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1402. It would be rude to make you go alone.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1403. Please let me know as soon as the date and location are decided.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1404. Even if it's Thursday, I'll try to make it work."
"Y-yeah..."
Madoka... still didn't have a good grasp of the situation, but she gave a vague nod. After seeing that, Tomoe let out a quick chuckle while flicking her on the forehead with her index finger.
"What are you clowning around for?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1406. Shouldn't you be happy?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1407. I just told you I approve of your marriage, you know."
"U-umm..."

"I completely understand... how you and the Chief feel.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1409. You're both serious about this, aren't you?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1410. I have no intention of getting in your way... so be happy, all right?"
Madoka was still dazed and confused by this unexpected development.
...She was expecting Tomoe to yell at her, or at the very least to flat out tell her "No."
Even so, hiding facts about meeting with Yamaoki's parents would feel like betraying her beloved older sister... so after thinking it over, she made the decision to at least stick to her principles.
And yet... to think she'd finally get the marriage approval she always longed for. It was cliche, but she was even starting to consider pinching her cheeks.
(But... I wonder why?)
She should've been happy.
Those were the words she always wanted to hear... but somehow, her heart ached.
The Tomoe that Madoka knew was a stricter individual. That's why she was so vehemently opposed to her marriage to Yamaoki. She was thinking about appearances, but more than that, about her future.
...Tomoe valued her family above all else. And after the death of her parents, that feeling grew even stronger.
So, when Madoka suffered psychological damage and began setting off on her own path... Tomoe went so far as to change her own course to watch over her...
"...I want you to be happy.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1412. To have a warm family, and to always have a pleasant time, as long as you live."
One day they got into a really big fight about marriage, and were on the verge of breaking off relations... but then Tomoe said that with a sorrowful expression and tears in her eyes.
"It might be fine now, but he's nearly double your age, right?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1414. He will no doubt pass on before you."
"...And if that happens, you'll be all alone, won't you?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1416. Are you really okay with that?"
"I-it's fine!@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1041. If we have children, I'll still be together with them. Plus, even if our time is short, I plan on building a happy family with him!"

"Then, do you intend to let your children experience the sorrow of losing a parent when they're still young?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1418. Have you really thought about how your children will feel?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1419. Are you okay with letting your own child go through the same experience we did?!"
And, she couldn't argue with that.
...She knew perfectly well that she and her sister had different definitions of happiness.
But... Tomoe was at least thinking about her happiness first and foremost.
Since her own family time was cut short, she wanted her new family to go on happily, for as long as possible. ...That was how Tomoe saw it.
That's why Madoka spent years persuading her, trying to get her to understand.
(...Still.)
Today, Tomoe seemed... calmer than she used to be.
It wasn't that she seemed to understand Madoka better... but rather, that it felt like the radiance of her life was dwindling.
In fact, even now while sitting on the sofa, she was trying to avoid any unnecessary use of strength, to alleviate her fatigue even if only a little.
(No way, Sis...?)
Perhaps Tomoe had gained the conviction to put her entire lifespan into these last few months.
Believing that she'd atone for causing that girl's death, and then...
Madoka violently shook her head to chase away the thoughts coming to mind. ...Ridiculous. It was too ominous to consider.
"Hmm? What?"
"Are you all right?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1048. Have you been getting enough sleep these days?"
"I've been getting sleep.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1422. I need to appear on TV from time to time, so I have to make sure my complexion stays reasonably healthy."
"...I see."
Tomoe had a bright smile on her face... but Madoka noticed.
Her makeup had gotten much thicker recently. And it wasn't out of fashion, but for other reasons.
She even using perfume, which she used to avoid for the sour taste. She was working hard to keep her exhaustion from showing.
On top of that, she had apparently begun tutoring a female student she recently met. ...It was impossibly hard work.
(But... even if I say anything, she probably won't listen.)
...Madoka recently heard the high-level details of the Oumaki incident from Fujita.
He said that Tomoe was apparently acquainted with the victim, and she was dreadfully upset at the crime scene.

"...I don't know all the details,@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1002. but Minai-san blamed herself, saying it was her fault the girl got involved in this case.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1003. ...I've never known her to be the sort of person to get that emotional."

"That's... not true.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1052. Fujita-san, that may have been your first time seeing it... but Sis was always like that.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1053. She just can't set aside her feelings... She's awkward that way."
"Madoka-san...?"
Even after hearing about the current situation from Yamaoki, and seeing her multiple times... Tomoe was always like this.
Whenever a wanted criminal commits an act of violence, claiming another victim.
Whenever a suspect falls into despair and takes their own life.
...She's always worrying and suffering, blaming her own inadequacies... driving herself to the edge.
Indeed... maybe she is slightly to blame. But when a crime occurs, it's undoubtedly the criminal's fault.
Yet, Tomoe couldn't bring herself to keep her personal feelings out of it.
And, she'd never show that side to her colleagues and subordinates... but there was a time she caught Tomoe drinking alone at a bar at the end of the day... and honestly, she was crying nonstop.
"...Fujita-san.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1055. I've thought something from time to time.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1056. That in all honesty... Sis might not be able to handle being a detective."
"...Madoka-san."

"Right now, how many people do you think are dying within the Kakiuchi Station jurisdiction area alone?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1058. Do you have any idea how many people are currently committing crimes?"
"Ten, twenty... maybe even a hundred people are grieving, suffering... and dying.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1060. If you feel sympathy or regret for every last one of them... your heart won't last."
"Plus, the police are human beings... They're not omnipotent.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1062. Even they make mistakes, and there are things they can't do."

"Even so, Sis always put in so much effort to respond to everyone, always moving here and there... exhausting herself, and enduring the pain.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1064. Even though she'll completely break... unless she can better set aside her feelings..."
...She already knew.
She herself was actually very selfish, and not opposed to making terse remarks that wouldn't reflect well on the police.
But even so... Madoka couldn't help but think.
Wouldn't it be better for her to live a smoother life?
Wouldn't it be better not to accept that level of responsibility to easily?
She'd worked hard enough to be allowed to do that, so she should be entitled to make that happen.
And yet... Tomoe always seemed to draw the short end of the stick.
Even if this NRIPS investigation project succeeds, Tomoe won't be the one to receive credit for it.
On the other hand, if it fails it'll definitely derail her career track.
To be blunt, solving a cold case in just three months wasn't impossible, it was reckless.
And yet... Tomoe placed her bet on that. Literally devoting her entire life as a police officer to make it happen.
Was it worth going that far?
Would anyone even recognize it if she did?
Madoka couldn't believe that they would.
Thinking that, she thought her sister poor and pitiable... and wanted to cry.
So... deep down, Madoka...
"What is it?"
"...@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1067.Good luck.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1068. If there's anything I can do to help, I'll do my best."
"Thanks.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1425. But before you do that, maybe you should take care of your own job."
"I know.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1070. ...Ahaha."
"Hehe..."
...She was deeply ashamed of her clumsiness, at being unable to bring herself to say anything more than that.
The truth is, she wanted to set Tomoe free right away.
She wanted to tell her sister, who always spent more time worrying about Madoka than herself, "that's enough." ...That was the main reason she decided to get married so soon.
And yet, she was being a huge burden on her because of her shallow feelings... it was so sad.
And even now, she couldn't do anything for her sister. ...Tears seemed to come flowing as she thought that, so she turned to look up at the ceiling.
The light from the fluorescent lamps was painful on her eyes...
At that moment.
"...Oh, there you are.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1008. I've been looking for you."
Two detectives stepped into the break room as he said that. They were Tomoe's subordinates, Fujita and Hanada.
...No, they're not her subordinates anymore.
"Oh, hello Madoka-san.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1010. Thank you for your hard work."
"Yeah, same to you."
Madoka noticed Fujita's sudden eye movements while returning his greeting. ...Seemed like that was her cue to leave.
"Alright, I need to head back."
"Yeah, I'll call you later tonight."
Sensing the atmosphere, Madoka lightly smiled and bowed her head to the two detectives, then fixed her hair as she quickly exited the room.
...After seeing that, Fujita hung a "cleaning in progress" sign on the break room door, locked it, and then took a seat on the sofa in front of Tomoe.
"...Thank you for your trouble.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1429. Do you have any new information?"

"No... sorry.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1012. The people connected to Nagisa Ozaki completely clammed up.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1013. They didn't even say a word.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1014. ...Oh, get me a cafe au lait too."
Fujita let out a sigh while loosening his tie. Then he tossed a hundred-yen coin to Hanada who was buying a can of coffee from the vending machine, before turning his head back.

"Everyone was terrified that the curse might strike them.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1016. Some of the victims from the Ibaraki incident moved even further away, and there were other families we lost track of."
"...Moved?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1431. Are they safe?"

"Aah, it's okay, I'm handling it from the other end.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1018. But, it's not a situation where I can exactly question them.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1019. Even though I'm trying to help, they call me a god of misfortune and slam their doors on me."
"We're completely on their bad side...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1433. Well, that's to be expected considering two people have died."
"There were even times when they sprinkled salt on me.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1002. ...Heads up."
Hanada chimed in while tossing a can of coffee to Fujita. Then, he dexterously pulled the tab back while pulling the police notebook from his breast pocket and laying it out.

"The folks at the Ibaraki school did nothing but repeat "I forgot," "don't remember," and "it's got nothing to do with me" over and over.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1004. Seems like everyone involved has been hit with a gag order and told how to respond."
"There's clearly a strong push to sweep the details of this assault under the rug."
"I see.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1435. By the way, Fujita-kun, what about Youko Hiranuma?"

"She came out totally clean too.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1021. She was apparently well behaved and a good student in her nursing school days.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1022. She began working at a normal hospital after graduation, and didn't have any problems or any unexcused absences."
"However, it seems she didn't have very many close friends, likely due to her overly serious demeanor.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1024. ...That's why her death was such a shock for the people involved."
"...In that case, the odds of it being drug related are low."
"Indeed. At worst, she was involved in smoking.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1026. But people in her position definitely have acquaintances, or maybe even romantic partners."
"...We considered that and tried getting in touch with Hiranuma's previous friends, but that ended up being tough."
Tomoe rested her chin on her folded hands, lost in thought.
...She was currently working for Public Relations, so she had no investigative authority of her own.
That's why she asked Fujita and Hanada to gather information on her behalf... but they didn't seem to be making any more progress than investigation HQ.

"Oh, that's right.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1029. There are two points that were omitted from the previous forensics report.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1030. It was discovered that both of Nagisa Ozaki's shoulders were dislocated."
"Both shoulders?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1439. Not just one?"

"Yes.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1032. There were scratches on her right shoulder, so we can probably infer that one was dislocated when she fell out of the vehicle.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1033. But there was also somewhat unnatural bruising on the left shoulder."
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1441.Seems that information wasn't in the original investigation report.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1442. I wonder why?"

"I think their original assessment was that both shoulders were dislocated in the fall.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1007. If someone fell from a car traveling at high speed, it certainly seems plausible for such an injury to happen."
Tomoe and Fujita both nodded at Hanada's explanation. In other words, after escaping from the first car, Nagisa may have been struck by another vehicle as she fell into the road...
"In addition to that, it seems one of her belongings, a bag of flower seeds, was found along the roadside.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1035. It must've been in her pocket and came out when she fell."
"Flower seeds... you say?"
Come to think of it, Nagisa did mention she was in the gardening club, so it wouldn't be that unusual for her to be carrying something like that around.
...But it's not something you carry around anywhere like potpourri, so there may be some specific meaning behind her carrying it...
"By the way, Hanada-kun, what's the status of Reina Ryuugu?"
"Right, about that... I did run into her once.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1009. I told her about Kouhei Sawamura and Nagisa Ozaki's deaths."
"Eh...? You said it that directly?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1037. Don't you think that's a bit overkill...?"

"I had no choice. Our investigation is completely deadlocked at the moment.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1011. Well, I was aware of the danger, but I thought it would be helpful if the momentum of the story got a reaction out of her.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1012. ...I apologize for acting without your permission."
"It's fine, I'm sure I would've been thinking the same thing.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1446. ...So, how did she take it?"
"...Not very strongly.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1014. It's more like she was genuinely apathetic to the news rather than just pretending it had nothing to do with her."
"Apathetic...?"
Tomoe furrowed her brows as she repeated that back. Hanada had a slightly averse look on his face as he nodded back to her, then continued.
"At the very least, she could have responded with "they were cursed" or "serves them right" to put on some pretense.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1016. But all she had to say was "Is that so?""
"To be honest, I was shocked that children today could be so quick to forget their old friends after moving to a new school."
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1449.Was there anything else?"

"Basically, it's a repeat of everything you and Detective Ooishi already investigated, Minai-san.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1019. ...Also, the fingerprints left on the gas cap of your patrol car were a positive match for Reina Ryuugu."
"It's probably just as you hypothesized. She may very well have been the one to drain the gas."
"...I see."
When Tomoe visited the Ryuugu household, someone intentionally siphoned the gas from her car.
Thanks to that, she had to head back to Okinomiya on foot. And on the way...
She recalled what happened back then.
The lock was forced open, but that wouldn't be difficult with appropriate tools. ...However the fact that it was done without making a sound suggested it was the work of a professional.
A girl with no specialized knowledge of cars was able to remove enough gas to kill the engine in such a short time frame...?
"I wonder if the girl could actually have managed that on her own?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1453. I still find it hard to swallow."
"In that case, should we bring her in for questioning?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1039. We could charge her with property damage, or obstruction of government duties... or any charges we can think of."
"If the fingerprints on the victim's belongings or the ticket match hers, the District Prosecutor's Office is likely to issue an arrest warrant."

"I had the same thought.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1022. ...However, according to a detective in Okinomiya, Reina Ryuugu is close friends with the daughter of the Sonozaki family, one of the Three Families orchestrating Hinamizawa."

"If we act too forcefully, they'll probably send a lawyer to the prefectural assembly to protect her.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1024. And if that happens, the entire village may cut us off, which would make it impossible to collect any more information."
"...That is a possibility."
Even so... the curse of Oyashiro-sama gives Hinamizawa a strong sense of unity, but the depth of that devotion is beyond normal.
Just what on earth makes the villagers there have such strong beliefs...?
"...Incidentally, what about the two of you?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1456. Do you believe in the curse?"

"Actually... I....@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1042. To be honest, I'm about 50/50 split on it.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1043. After all, this case is so out of the blue, and not enough of the pieces fit together.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1044. So there have been times where I've thought it would be so easy if I could just assume it's the work of a curse."
For Fujita to go that far made it clear how difficult the situation was. Even Tomoe herself was tempted to suspect the involvement of a supernatural element.
"What about you, Hanada-kun?"

"Well.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1026. Really, I think humans are far more frightening than ghosts.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1027. Unlike creatures we don't know for sure even exist, human emotions are plain to see."
"That's surprising.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1460. For someone who visited Hinamizawa, I'd expect you to be more conscious of that."

"Well, I did get a strange impression.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1029. But it wasn't so much fear as it was irritation.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1030. It was like so many eyes were always staring at me like an outsider. It felt unpleasant."
"Huh...? Hanada-kun, didn't you travel with Ooishi-san?"
"Huh?@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1032. Right... about that."
...Hanada said that he initially planned to take Tomoe's advice and cooperate with Ooishi of Okinomiya Station, but unfortunately he was away on a business trip, so he had no choice but to go alone.
He had heard about how Tomoe was attacked there, and felt it would be a bit careless... but at the time, he was convinced there was no other option.
Hanada had no prior knowledge of Hinamizawa, so even though he was told about the danger he likely had no idea what it actually entailed...
(...Next time, maybe I'll try to go meet her.)
A phone call would be sufficient, but it's far easier to get a feel for people when you can meet up with them and speak directly. Perhaps she'd be able to obtain some new information.
Of course, she was reluctant to ever set foot in Hinamizawa again.
"...Oh, it's almost time.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1464. I need to head to the TV station."
"Another interview?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1046. That must be tough."
"It's only a prep meeting today, so it's no big deal.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1466. Well, I may need to go out and grab a drink afterwards."
"You need to get some rest once in a while.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1048. If you collapsed right now, all the NRIPS guys and the people in the field would clash, and this project would disappear."
...There was no hint of levity in Fujita's words. Some of their investigators had already openly complained about the arrogant, callous attitude of the NRIPS researchers.
"I'll slow down once it's all over.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1468. Until then, I need to keep running as hard as I can."
...But, where was she headed?
And after it was all over, would she feel like she accomplished something...? Or would she be left feeling empty?
Even Tomoe herself didn't know for sure.
"Alright, let me know if you make any progress."
"Roger."
"Leave it to us."
Tomoe parted ways with Fujita and Hanada, intending to head to the TV station by car, so she headed out the main entrance and into the nearby parking lot.
The sun had already set, and the veil of darkness had already been draped over the surrounding area.
And, out there...
Looking closely, there was a man sitting on a large stone, smoking a cigarette.
He was wrapped in thick shadows, so she probably would have never noticed him if not for the bright red flame.
...He seemed to be killing time by blowing smoke circles. She was all too familiar with that gesture.
"...Arakawa-san?"
"Hmm...?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1002. Ah, Chief Inspector Minai-san.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1003. Hello."
When the man noticed Tomoe, he stamped out his cigarette, then smiled and aimlessly walked toward her.
His lightly soiled jacket and shabby-looking jeans made him look like a vagrant.
"Oh my, this is perfect.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1005. I was wondering how I should contact you, so this hits the nail right on the head."
"Smoking in a place like this? Are you trying to end up in police custody?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1473. Hehe..."
While somewhat exasperatedly shrugging her shoulders, Tomoe picked up the cigarette butt that had fallen to the ground and tossed it in a nearby sewer grate.
...Ryuuichi Arakawa.
A freelance journalist who covered a wide range of topics, from politics to the sex industry.
He also gets a lot of hate for his brute-force interview style that ignores press conventions. The rumors about him are not pleasant.

Even so, he also a key "informant" reporting directly to Director Maeno in the Public Relations office. He was deeply familiar with how to gather information from both the underground and the upper echelons.
"Did you need something from me?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1475. You should've called ahead to let me know."
"I shudder at the idea of entering a police station.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1007. Some of the folks there see me as an enemy, so I'd prefer to avoid it if at all possible."
"Couldn't we have just discussed it over the phone?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1477. I gave you my direct number, didn't I?"
"...Haha, I'm aware of that.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1009. Basically, it's that sort of business."
Tomoe's eyes suddenly narrowed and sharpened.

...In short, it meant that he didn't want anyone to know about his contact with Tomoe. It was something she'd rather not consider, but given the current situation there was a possibility of eavesdroppers even within the police station.
"...Anyway, can we discuss it on the move?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1480. I need to head to the Kakiuchi TV station right after this."
"Yes, of course.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1011. In that case, can you drop me off at the Kakiuchi airport?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1012. I'm a bit short to make taxi fare... Tahaha."
Arakawa shamelessly said that while scratching his head.
Though shocked by his impudence, Tomoe shrugged her shoulders and said "okay," then walked over to where her car was parked.
The bright headlights of Tomoe's car parted the veil of darkness as they gently drove forward.
The traffic volume was actually fairly reasonable, and not stressful at all. She'd likely be able to arrive at the TV station well in advance, even after a quick stop at the airport.
Before long, Tomoe noticed the traffic light about 100 meters ahead turn red, so she made an abrupt left turn. Then, the car pulled into a slightly narrow road and she stepped on the gas.
"...Huh?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1014. Isn't the airport a straight shot down the highway?"
"This is a shortcut.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1483. The main road has too many lights you can catch.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1484. You can save maybe five to ten minutes going this way."
"Wow, I had no idea.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1016. You're well informed. As expected of a local police officer."
Arakawa was giving honest praise... but Tomoe was confident that nobody at Kakiuchi Station, not even the locals, were that intimately familiar with the area's geography.
...After the death of her parents, Tomoe had been regularly traveling to Kakiuchi airport, since her father was investigating something there when he was still alive.
While visiting every year, the roads in the surrounding area had gotten significantly brighter.
And in addition to that, she would often imagine how elaborate siege operations would play out if there was ever a criminal inside the airport.
Although, those efforts hadn't proven useful even once in the past 12 years...
"...The Public Security mark seems to have been called off."
Shortly after merging onto the more heavily trafficked highway, Arakawa's sudden words caused Tomoe to lightly catch her breath.
He glanced at Tomoe's sudden change in facial expression, then continued in a monotone voice.
"I still don't know the reason.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1019. ...But the information came from credible sources."
"...Is that so?"
...Tomoe felt there was something strange happening around her recently, so she asked Arakawa to find out what it was.
And from him, she learned that investigators from MPD Public Security and Personnel Affairs had been following her.

"If the mark has been rescinded, well, I suppose that's a good thing...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1488. But it's a creepy story for them to do that and then call it off for no reason though.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1489. ...What did Director Maeno have to say about it?"

"The director... doesn't know exactly what caused it, either.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1021. Even though I asked an acquaintance in Public Security about it, I didn't learn anything since they're from a different line.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1022. But it didn't seem like they were trying to hide anything."
"...Akiyama-san was saying the same thing."
Commissioner Akiyama of the Aichi prefectural police Public Security bureau was an old acquaintance of Maeno, so he was willing to share intel with Tomoe within the scope of his duties.
But if it was a line of investigation even he still couldn't get a handle on, it meant this was something very deep within the top-secret intelligence agency...
There are two scenarios where Public Security will mark an individual.
The first is a subject clearly intending to commit an act against national interests.
And the second is when dealing with people connected to subjects of the first type.
In other words...

"I wonder if the case I'm investigating has some connection to Public Security?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1493. And the fact that they called off the mark means they've made some sort of progress, or there was a change of circumstances..."

"Well, you could say so.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1024. Apparently the culprit you've been pursuing... has considerable backing.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1025. It's not just as simple as killing a person.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1026. There are a lot of strings attached."
A flash of inspiration suddenly went through Tomoe's head.
Half a year ago, Yamaoki had Tomoe transferred to the Public Relations Office.

...She later learned from Director Maeno that it wasn't just disciplinary action against her for assaulting Second Division Section Chief Okabe, but also because Deputy Chief Hata was pursuing liability against her.

But... the truth is, they likely had another objective. Because they could have easily transferred her to the First Division of Aichi prefectural police, or the Chubu region police department... or a plethora of other options.
Nevertheless, they had her moved to a department with no jurisdiction for criminal investigation... to show someone that she had been removed from the investigation.
In which case... who is that someone?
What on earth was Yamaoki thinking, moving her away from criminal investigation...?
"Anyway... even though the mark is gone, I think you still need to be careful.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1028. Even I don't want to see an acquaintance of mine get caught up in an unfortunate incident."
"Hehe... very good.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1496. Do you think I'm one to feel grateful for my position while dying in the line of duty?"
"You're a confident person.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1030. ...Well, I figured you'd say that."
Arakawa sighed and scratched his head, with a frightened look on his face.
...She had a cute face, and she really was a stouthearted girl. That was probably why a shrewd man in the National Police Agency like Maeno was so interested in introducing her.
"Oh, right.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1498. What happened to the person from that case?"

"That case?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1032. ...Aah, you mean her?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1033. Don't worry about it. I carried out all my responsibilities like a good boy last week.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1033a. ...Pardon me."
Arakawa pulled a box of cigarettes out of his breast pocket as he said that, then put one in his mouth and lit it.
He thought it would be okay to smoke with her consent, but then he seemed to get embarrassed and reconsider, until Tomoe urged him to go ahead.

"Honestly, the idea of people getting involved in this sort of yakuza business while having a family feels like a bad joke or something.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1035. When your kid says they want to go to elementary school, what choice do you have?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1036. You accept your fate and let them join the family."

"Ahahaha, congratulations on that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1500. But your wife sure is something.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1501. Even though your child is growing up, you don't make any effort to contact them?"
"Hmm... well, she thought they might just be getting in my way.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1038. She's way too considerate.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1039. I'm not that cold a person though, I'd help if asked."
"...Well, if you never talk to one another then nothing will ever get started."
"Absolutely.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1041. To say nothing even when you're concerned is just complacency in the end."
"When you're really worried about someone, they won't suddenly change their attitude unless it's something extreme, after all.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1043. I think it was a good lesson for her too."
"I'd say that applies to both of you... right?"
"Hahaha!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1045. You've got me there."
Arakawa had a lively smile on his face as he said that, rubbing his cigarette out in the ashtray.
...Tomoe secretly smiled to herself, thinking his facial expression looked much more refreshed than before.

"Next month... they're conducting an air show at the Self-Defense Force base next to Kakiuchi Airport.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1047. I was planning to go to feign the idea of spending time with the family.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1048. ...Bring my wife and child. The three of us."

"Ahaha, that sounds nice.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1505. Fighter jets performing aerial acrobatics isn't exactly something you can see in Tokyo.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1506. I think your child would be delighted."
The runways at the Kakiuchi airport are occasionally shared with the SDF. So, the air force garrison and base facilities hold an air show every year in June and invite foreign forces.
"Oh, but I heard that viewing tickets are a bit pricy.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1508. Wasn't it tough to get them?"
"Haha, I received a little help from Maeno-san on that."
Tomoe gave a deeply understanding nod to that. ...Now that he mentioned it, Maeno did say that he could use his Public Relations connections to get tickets to anything.

"But why is it that boys always have a fascination with fast vehicles like airplanes and motorcycles?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1510. Even Hanada-kun at our station fits that bill, but I still don't understand it."

"Hoh?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1051. Then maybe next time we should go cruising together.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1052. The land and sky are great, but driving recklessly on the sea is pretty fun too, y'know?"
"And now you're flirting with infidelity?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1512. Ahahaha!"
She turned the wheel to the right as she said that, and arrived at a wide-open, straight road.
The Kakiuchi airport terminal building could be seen straight ahead.
"Alright, right here is good.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1054. Thank you for the lift."
"You're welcome.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1514. If you learn any new information, please let me know."
"Got it.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1056. And give my regards to Director Maeno.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_araF1057. ...Anyway, see you later."
Arakawa held his hand up with two fingers extended and waved, as he disappeared into the airport lobby.
After watching him go, Tomoe got back in the car, then stopped and gazed at an airplane as it flew across the sky up ahead.
""Kakiuchi Airport, June, Wednesday. Go.""
She repeated to herself the words written on the last page of her father's notebook.
Twelve years ago, Tomoe's father was a detective at Public Security, trying to investigate something at this very airport.
And now, Public Security had marked her to monitor her movements.
Maybe it was just a coincidence... but maybe it had something to do with the case her father was pursuing?
Was it the truth she was approaching?
Or was it the hour of death?
Tomoe herself wasn't sure.
